feat: add comprehensive benchmarks and performance optimizations

- Add bench_test.go for all packages (table, csv, etl, schema)
  with 1k/10k/100k size variants and init() fixture caching
- Add cmd/benchsummary: parses go test -bench output, groups by
  function, averages across -count runs, shows scaling factor
- Add bench.sh: orchestrates benchmark runs with -parallel,
  -count=N and -filter=PATTERN flags
- Fix csv bench_test.go: Write/ToString/Write_Separators were
  writing a hardcoded 2-row table instead of sized test data
- Fix MutableTransformParallel: replace copy-on-write approach with
  direct in-place parallel writes per disjoint row chunk; eliminates
  n per-row allocations, parallel now faster than sequential at 100k
- Fix MedianCol: replace sort.Float64s (O(n log n)) with randomized
  quickselect (O(n) expected); 51% faster at 100k, scaling ×105 vs ×211
- Update .gitignore: exclude benchsummary binary and bench output files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add GitHub Pages deployment — play in the browser

Web deployment using Pyodide (Python WASM) + xterm.js terminal:
- docs/index.html: landing page with xterm.js terminal
- docs/main.js: terminal I/O and worker communication
- docs/worker.js: Pyodide loader, stdin/stdout/sleep bridge
- docs/coi-serviceworker.js: COOP/COEP headers for SharedArrayBuffer
- docs/style.css: dark retro terminal theme
- .github/workflows/pages.yml: CI copies *.py to docs/game/ and deploys

Architecture: Web Worker runs Pyodide with SharedArrayBuffer for
synchronous input(). Colorama is mocked with raw ANSI codes that
xterm.js interprets natively. time.sleep uses Atomics.wait timeout.

commit.message:
Add contract tests for proto file sync and generated code freshness (#79)

tests/contract/proto_sync_test.go:

- TestContract_ExtensionProtoLinkedToSource: verifies that
  apix-vscode/proto/apix.proto correctly references the Go source of
  truth at pkg/api/proto/apix.proto. Handles both the symlink-based
  workflow (current) and a copy-based workflow, catching three failure
  modes: broken symlink, symlink pointing to the wrong target, and a
  stale file copy with drifted content.

- TestContract_GeneratedGoMatchesProto: regenerates pkg/api/generated/
  in a temp dir via protoc and diffs output against committed files,
  catching cases where the proto was edited without re-running protoc.
  Auto-skips when protoc / protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc are not
  in PATH.

Closes #79

commit.message:
fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#406)

* fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#255)

The leaflet-routing-machine helper _leftOrRight, used when converting
on/off ramp steps, was purely binary: anything that didn't contain
'left' was returned as 'Right'. This meant an 'on ramp straight'
maneuver ended up with modifier 'Right', rendering a right-turn arrow
even though the instruction text said 'straight'.

Monkey-patch _leftOrRight on the router instance so that modifiers
containing neither 'left' nor 'right' (e.g. 'straight') are passed
through unchanged. 'straight' then becomes the 'Straight' modifier and
getIconName correctly maps it to the continue (straight) arrow icon.

Also add a dedicated test suite covering all modifier cases including
the straight regression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add upstream issue link to _leftOrRight monkey-patch comment

  risking the implementation and test drifting out of sync.
- Tests were re-implementing the helper locally, so they would pass even

src/index.js calls applyPatches(router); the test imports leftOrRight

commit.message:
gps: 14 Hz output rate, 1 Hz PPS timepulse

GPS output rate 16->14 Hz (GPS_NOMINAL_MS 63->71 ms): at 16 Hz the
chip was missing ~20-24 samples per 256-sample buffer (~14.6 Hz
actual with multi-constellation). 14 Hz sits safely below the
chip's reliable output rate, eliminating fill samples.

PPS timepulse 25->1 Hz (period 1_000_000 us): the timepulse is only
used for RTC clock sync via GPIO interrupt, so 1 Hz is sufficient
and reduces unnecessary ISR load.

commit.message:
refactor: replace prediction engine with calibrated GBM model

BREAKING: Prediction engine completely overhauled to fix absurd scenarios
(e.g. NVDA predicted at $1000/share for bull scenario).

Root causes fixed:
- HMM with only 252 days of data produced unreliable regime estimates
- No bounds on p5/p95 percentiles → extreme values passed to UI
- √T extrapolation (scalePercentiles) amplified errors exponentially
- Raw 1-year historical mean used as future expected return

New architecture:
- Primary model: Calibrated GBM with Student-t quantiles (ν=5)
  - Closed-form percentiles, no Monte Carlo needed
  - Drift shrinkage: blend historical μ with 8% equity prior (w=dataYears/10)
  - Damped volatility for horizons >2yr (mean-reversion à la Fama & French)
  - Hard sanity bounds: [-80%,+100%]/year, [-95%,+1000%] total
- Secondary: Block bootstrap for short horizons (≤6 months)
- HMM demoted to informational regime display only
- GARCH retired from ensemble (no value beyond ~20 trading days)

Expected results (NVDA ~$140, σ≈50%, 12mo):
- Before: Bear -70%, Bull +150% (or worse)
- After: Bear ~-38%, Bull ~+58%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(mcp): add Streamable HTTP and SSE transport support (#580)

## Summary

The RedisVL MCP server previously only supported stdio transport, which
meant it could only be used by local MCP clients that spawn the server
as a subprocess. This PR adds support for **Streamable HTTP** and
**SSE** transports, enabling remote MCP clients (such as no-code
platforms and hosted AI tools) to connect to the server over the
network.

## What Changed

### CLI (`redisvl/cli/mcp.py`)

Three new flags added to `rvl mcp`:

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--transport` | `stdio` | Transport protocol: `stdio`, `sse`, or
`streamable-http` |
| `--host` | `127.0.0.1` | Bind address (used with `sse` and
`streamable-http` only) |
| `--port` | `8000` | Bind port (used with `sse` and `streamable-http`
only) |

The `_serve` method now forwards transport, host, and port to FastMCP
`run_async()`. For stdio, host and port are omitted.

### Docs

- **`docs/user_guide/how_to_guides/mcp.md`**: Expanded "Start the
Server" with examples for all three transports, added a CLI flags table,
and added a "Connect a Remote MCP Client" section with URL patterns and
a JSON config example.
- **`docs/concepts/mcp.md`**: Updated transport description to reflect
all three options.
- **`docs/user_guide/index.md`**: Updated the MCP card tagline.
- **`README.md`**: Added Streamable HTTP example to CLI and MCP Server
sections, added transport bullet point to the server feature list.

## Usage

### Start over Streamable HTTP

```bash
uvx --from redisvl[mcp] rvl mcp --config /path/to/mcp.yaml --transport streamable-http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
```

Then point your remote MCP client at: `http://<host>:8000/mcp`

### Start over SSE

```bash
uvx --from redisvl[mcp] rvl mcp --config /path/to/mcp.yaml --transport sse --port 9000
```

Then point your remote MCP client at: `http://<host>:9000/sse`

### Start over stdio (unchanged default)

```bash
uvx --from redisvl[mcp] rvl mcp --config /path/to/mcp.yaml
```

### Remote MCP client config example

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "redisvl": {
      "url": "http://192.168.1.10:8000/mcp",
      "transport": "streamable-http"
    }
  }
}
```

## Tests

### Unit tests (`tests/unit/test_cli_mcp.py`)

7 new tests covering:

- Streamable HTTP transport wired through `run_async`
- SSE transport wired through `run_async`
- stdio default omits host/port kwargs
- Invalid transport rejected by argparse
- Fallback `run()` path forwards HTTP transport kwargs
- Fallback `run()` path for stdio omits host/port
- Help output documents transport flags
- SSE through fallback `run()` path
- Default host/port values for HTTP transports

### Integration tests (`tests/integration/test_mcp/test_transport.py`)

4 new tests covering:

- In-process FastMCP Client discovers and calls tools
- Full HTTP end-to-end: server starts on a free port with
`streamable-http`, client connects via HTTP URL, lists tools, calls
`search-records`
- Read-only mode hides `upsert-records` when accessed over HTTP
- SSE end-to-end: server starts with `sse` transport, client connects
via SSE URL, lists tools, calls `search-records`

All 126 MCP-related tests pass (104 existing unit + 18 CLI unit + 4
integration).

## Review Findings Addressed

3 actionable findings were fixed in a follow-up commit:

1. **TCP readiness probe**: Replaced fixed `asyncio.sleep(1.0)` with a
`_wait_for_port()` helper that polls every 50ms with a 5s timeout.
Faster and more reliable on slow CI.
2. **Narrowed exception handling**: Changed `except (CancelledError,
Exception): pass` to `except CancelledError: pass` in test cleanup
blocks so real server errors surface.
3. **SSE integration test added**: Added
`test_server_serves_over_sse_transport` so SSE coverage matches the
docstring claim. Updated docstring accordingly.

3 findings were triaged as skip:
- TOCTOU in `_find_free_port()`: accepted pattern, no API support for
alternative
- CLI help missing `--host` in one example: covered in full docs
- Fallback `run()` blocking for HTTP: theoretical only, `run_async`
always exists on `RedisVLMCPServer`

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds new network-accessible MCP transports (Streamable HTTP/SSE) and
exposes bind host/port configuration, which can broaden attack surface
if deployed without authentication; behavior is otherwise largely wiring
and documentation plus tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Enables running the RedisVL MCP server over **Streamable HTTP** and
**SSE** in addition to the existing stdio default, allowing remote MCP
clients to connect.
> 
> Updates `rvl mcp` to accept `--transport`, `--host`, and `--port` and
forwards these into the server run path (including the fallback `run()`
lifecycle) while omitting host/port for stdio.
> 
> Expands docs/README with transport-specific run examples, client
connection URLs, and an explicit warning that HTTP/SSE endpoints are
unauthenticated by default, and adds unit + integration coverage
validating tool discovery/calls over HTTP and read-only behavior.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit
ac7dff0c27bca8d2768e55eaddd8e9b0ef32359f. Bugbot is set up for automated
code reviews on this repo. Configure
[here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commit.message:
fix: clock-set spin loop — store now not egps.time in state

set_from_egps stored egps.time (GPS PVT timestamp) in LocationState
after a successful RTC set. If EGPS_TIME was stale (GPS time >10s in
the past), (now - egps.time) > LOCATION_DIFF was true again on the
very next call, causing an infinite re-trigger loop.

Fix: store 'now' (wall-clock at time of set) so the 10s cooldown is
measured from when the set actually happened. Also rate-limit the
NaiveDateTime::from_timestamp_millis failure path.

commit.message:
feat: Build improvements (#142)

Addresses two recent feedback items:
- zig pollutes the user's .cache/zig directory, which may grow large
- Selene manifests take too long to write

The first item is managed by providing zig with a cache dir, which
subsequently sets `ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR` in the subprocess environment.

The second is managed by providing a `complete_manifest` bool flag to
`build()`. When True, the manifest aims to be a complete picture of the
build stages, for use with sharing and diagnosis. When False (default),
less information is written to the manifest. At current this reduced
information is in the form of stripping input artifact or intermediate
artifacts in the manifest, keeping only the final binary.

* feat: Implement TC Status message and J1939-81 compliance fixes

This commit addresses the TC/VT startup order issue where the Task Controller
would not be discovered by implements when starting after the Virtual Terminal.

Key Changes:

- Add TC Status message (PGN 0xCB00, Command 0x0E) transmitted every 2 seconds
  per ISO 11783-10 B.8.1 for periodic TC announcement to late-joining clients

- Implement identify_task_controller() callback to respond to VT queries
  with TC number display (ISO 11783-10 B.5.4/B.5.5)

- Add J1939-81 section 4.4.4.1 compliance: 250ms delay after address claim
  before transmitting other messages for both TC and TECU

- Add TECU address claim validation with proper error handling and cleanup
  if TECU fails to claim its fixed address (address 128)

- Add timestamped logging (HH:MM:SS.mmm) to all application and library logs
  for better debugging and traceability

- Fix lambda capture and send_can_message API compatibility issues

Fixes:
- TC not discovered when starting after VT
- TECU crashes due to AgIsoStack++ library update requirement
- Identify Task Controller callback

* Apply suggestions from code review

fix(seo): use dynamic import for prisma in metadata to prevent build crash

Static import of prisma in layout.tsx caused metadata generation
to fail when DATABASE_URL was unavailable, breaking the social
preview entirely. Using dynamic import with try/catch ensures
graceful fallback.
